mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth captured by PAYEMS reflects labor income expansion that fuels consumer spending and corporate earnings; sustained increases in this series drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an emerging goods-sector contraction that reduces aggregate profit growth and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO has declined for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls reveal a coordinated demand and labor market expansion that boosts corporate revenue visibility and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase month-over-month do not produce positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion characterized by a federal funds rate above 0.5%, a decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates that labor market momentum is stalling under tightening financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, months with declining PAYEMS do not produce negative 5-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1300 thousand units demonstrates robust residential construction activity that generates downstream demand for materials, employment, and credit, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST exceeds 1300 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the consumer price index signal a disinflationary environment that lowers expected nominal discount rates and real debt burdens, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL has declined for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales compress the probability of a broad economic contraction and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint monthly increases in INDPRO and RSAFS do not produce positive 60-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises equity discount rates and corporate debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.
